entries.intro: There's been bickering down the street over who gets to use the well first in the mornings. It's gotten loud.
details: Started with two families, but now half the street has an opinion about it.
Someone put a sign up saying to take turns. Someone else tore it down. Very mature.
reactions: It'll blow over. These things always do. Just takes longer than you'd like.

entries.intro: A couple of farmers outside town are in a real stew over a fence line. Each says the other moved it.
details: I've heard both sides and they're both completely sure they're right. You know how that goes.
One of them brought out an old deed and the other one laughed at it. That didn't help.
reactions: It's always the small things that get people the angriest. Nobody fights like neighbors.

statCheck.pass: Fence disputes usually come down to whoever set the posts last. Ground shifts, posts lean. The line drifts over the years.
statCheck.fail: I don't know anything about property lines. I just know they're both grumpy about it.

entries.intro: Heard there was a shouting match in the square yesterday. Something about a borrowed cart that came back broken.
details: The one who broke it says it was already cracked. The owner says it was fine when it left.
A wheel came off, apparently. Could happen to any cart if you hit a big enough rock.
reactions: Lending things out always ends this way. I stopped doing it years ago.

entries.intro: There's been some grumbling around here about someone's goats getting into other people's gardens. Same story every year.
details: The goat owner insists they're penned up. The evidence suggests otherwise.
I saw one of them myself. Standing in a flowerbed, chewing away without a care in the world.
reactions: Hard to stay angry at a goat, though. They don't know any better. The owner, on the other hand.

statCheck.pass: That pen has a weak spot where the lower rail is rotted through. I noticed it weeks ago but it's not my place to say.
statCheck.fail: Goats are goats. If there's a way out, they'll find it.

entries.intro: Actually, that disagreement between the neighbors seems to have sorted itself out. I saw the two of them sharing a drink yesterday.
details: One of them made a peace offering. A fresh pie, I think. Hard to stay mad at someone holding a pie.
They were laughing about it by the end. Funny how that works.
reactions: Good to see. Life's too short for grudges over fences and goats.
